project.name: AcuScan AR
project.description: Web-based AR wellness guide that overlays acupressure point markers on a live hand/wrist camera feed.
project.persona: Someone with wrist or hand pain looking for quick relief at their desk. Not technical. Has 2 minutes. Using a phone they're already holding. Wants to feel helped, not confused. Will not read instructions.

session.handoff_note: Stack decision confirmed: web-based. TF.js MediaPipe hands. Next is scaffolding hand tracking with camera input.

decisions.question: Use WebXR or Canvas overlay for AR rendering?
decisions.recommendation: Canvas overlay â€” broader mobile browser support, simpler implementation for MVP
decisions.confidence_reason: WebXR has spotty iOS support. Canvas overlay works everywhere.

proactive_questions.question: If hand tracking lags on older phones â€” prioritize speed or visual accuracy?
proactive_questions.context: TF.js MediaPipe runs slower on budget devices. May need to reduce landmark polling rate.

proactive_questions.question: Should point descriptions be text overlaid on screen or a separate info panel below the camera?
proactive_questions.context: Affects UI layout significantly. Text on camera = cleaner but harder to read. Panel below = easier to read but splits attention.

proactive_questions.question: Eventually monetize this or keep it free/personal?
proactive_questions.context: Affects whether we add any tracking, accounts, or premium features later.
proactive_questions.unlocks: Architecture decisions around accounts and data

autonomous_backlog.task: Research TF.js MediaPipe Hands browser compatibility â€” document which mobile browsers work
Define acupoints data model â€” LI4, PC6, HT7, TW4, LU9 with landmark indices and descriptions
Scaffold single HTML file with camera access and TF.js MediaPipe import
Build overlay canvas that draws a dot on one hardcoded landmark point to verify tracking works

assumption_log.assumption: Users have a modern smartphone with front-facing camera
App is personal use, not clinical â€” no medical disclaimers needed beyond basic note
No login or account required for MVP

done_means: Camera opens on mobile Chrome without install
Hand detected within 2 seconds of appearing in frame
All 5 acupoints show as colored dots on correct landmark positions
Overlay stays locked to hand during slow movement

session_log.next_action: Resolve WebXR vs Canvas decision then scaffold hand tracking

project.name: FB/eBay Arbitrage Pulse
project.description: Automated monitor that scrapes Facebook Marketplace for mispriced items, cross-references eBay sold listings for margin analysis, and delivers a daily digest of top flip opportunities.
project.persona: Active reseller (Joe) who knows what sells but doesn't have time to scroll Marketplace manually for hours. Wants a 30-second morning decision: which 5 items are worth pursuing today.

session.handoff_note: New project. No code yet. First session: define geographic target, keyword list, and scraping approach before writing anything.

decisions.question: What geographic area to monitor â€” Tennessee local, Illinois local, or both?
decisions.recommendation: Both with separate digests â€” you travel between both regularly
decisions.confidence_reason: Depends on where you actually source inventory. User knows best.

decisions.question: Scraping approach â€” Apify Actor or direct browser automation via Chrome extension?
options: Chrome extension automation (free, may break with FB updates)
decisions.recommendation: Start with Chrome extension automation â€” free, and we already have it set up
decisions.confidence_reason: Facebook actively fights scrapers. Apify is more reliable long-term but costs money. Prove concept free first.

proactive_questions.question: What categories do you want to monitor? (e.g. tools, electronics, collectibles, furniture, vintage)
proactive_questions.context: Keyword list drives everything. More specific = less noise = better digest.

proactive_questions.question: Minimum profit margin to include in digest â€” e.g. only show items with 40%+ margin?
proactive_questions.context: Sets the filter threshold for the margin analysis step.

proactive_questions.question: Digest delivery format â€” Markdown file in Drive, email, or dashboard card?
proactive_questions.context: Markdown in Drive is simplest. Email requires SMTP setup. Dashboard card integrates with studio.

autonomous_backlog.task: Research eBay Sold Listings API â€” document how to query sold prices for a given item keyword
Design margin calculation formula â€” (eBay avg sold - FB asking price) / FB asking price
Draft initial keyword list based on Joe's known resale categories

assumption_log.assumption: Facebook Marketplace is primary source â€” not OfferUp, Craigslist, etc.
eBay sold listings are the price reference benchmark

done_means: At least one geographic area monitored
At least 3 keyword categories active
Margin calculated correctly against eBay sold data
Digest file generated with top 5 results
Results are real listings not test data

session_log.next_action: Answer geographic and category decisions, then design scraping approach

current.name: CTW
current.handoff: Phase 2 complete. Stress test runner working. CÃ©leste validated at deployment quality. Next: Phase 3 day structure and time engine, plus real character portrait images to replace SVG placeholders.
blockers: Real portrait images not yet generated â€” using SVG placeholders
Trait activation detector needs calibration against more test runs
comfort bar still overcorrects downward under pressure

current.sessionSummary: Full Phase 2 built including story loader, provider dropdown, stress test battery, and character library. CÃ©leste test run validated core architecture â€” appeasement resistance held through pressure phase. Three scoring bugs fixed.

project.name: HiBid Analyzer
project.description: Browser tool that analyzes auction lot images via Gemini API, estimates resale value, and saves notes to HiBid watchlist.
project.persona: Active auction bidder (Joe) sitting at a computer during a live HiBid auction. Needs value estimates fast. No time to research manually. Wants notes saved automatically so he can bid with confidence.

session.handoff_note: Gemini API image analysis works. Blocked on HiBid textarea DOM automation. Chrome extension is now available â€” this changes the approach entirely. First session should attempt textarea interaction via extension before any other approach.

decisions.question: Now that Chrome extension is available â€” attempt textarea via extension first or continue investigating DOM approach?
options: Extension first â€” highest probability of success
DOM investigation â€” understand root cause first
decisions.recommendation: Extension first â€” Claude in Chrome has direct page access, bypasses the DOM isolation problem entirely
decisions.confidence_reason: Extension confirmed working on first try per user. Direct browser access eliminates the automation layer that was blocking.

proactive_questions.question: Should analysis run on all lots automatically or only when you click a button on a specific lot?
proactive_questions.context: Auto-run = more convenient but costs more API calls. Manual trigger = you control what gets analyzed.

proactive_questions.question: What format do you want the watchlist notes in? (e.g. 'Est: $45-65 | Condition: Good | Items: ceramic vase')
proactive_questions.context: Note format determines what Gemini is asked to return. Standardized format makes notes scannable during live bidding.
proactive_questions.unlocks: Gemini prompt design and note writing logic

proactive_questions.question: Should this work on mobile Chrome or desktop only?
proactive_questions.context: Mobile adds complexity but useful if bidding from phone at estate sales.

autonomous_backlog.task: Document current Gemini API integration â€” what prompt is used, what format is returned, what works
Research HiBid watchlist page structure â€” document what the notes field looks like in the DOM
Design standardized note format for watchlist entries based on proactive_002 answer

decision_log.decision: Use Gemini API for image analysis
decision_log.reason: Already integrated and working. No reason to switch.

assumption_log.assumption: Chrome (not Opera) will be used for HiBid sessions
No backend needed â€” runs locally in browser

reusable_findings.finding: Gemini API base64 inline image analysis pattern â€” works reliably for lot image processing

done_means: Open HiBid auction lot in Chrome
Analysis runs on lot image
Value estimate appears in correct format
Note is saved to HiBid watchlist without manual copy/paste
Note persists after page refresh

completed: Chrome extension confirmed working on first try
blockers: Textarea DOM automation unresolved â€” extension approach now primary
session_log.next_action: Attempt textarea write via Chrome extension on live HiBid page

project.name: Talent Insight Engine (Job Match Platform)
project.description: AI-powered job matching platform. Two sides: (1) Job Seeker â€” narrative interview generates behavioral profile, matched against live job pool. (2) Employer â€” browse candidate profiles with AI alignment scoring. Scraping engine runs two-tier crawl: monthly broad discovery + daily targeted delta fetch with JIT dead-link validation.
project.persona: Job seeker tired of irrelevant results, scam listings, and dead links. Wants a ranked list of real, attainable opportunities matched to their actual behavioral profile â€” not just keywords. Also: employer who wants behavioral fit data, not just credentials.

tech_stack.ai: OpenAI GPT-4o via Replit proxy â€” NEEDS MIGRATION to Anthropic API
tech_stack.auth: Replit Auth (OpenID Connect) â€” NEEDS MIGRATION to standard auth
tech_stack.scraping: NOT YET BUILT â€” architecture designed, ready to implement

working: Narrative interview â€” story-based questions for job seekers
GPT-4o generates: aiSummary, employerSummary, workingTendencies, internalTraits
Effort reduction flags (spam detection, input quality)
Employer portal â€” browse candidate profiles
AI alignment scoring â€” fitBand + signals between candidate and employer intent
PostgreSQL schema â€” users, sessions, profiles, conversations, messages
Landing page, seeker flow, employer flow
not_built: Match ranking between seeker profile and job pool
needs_migration: Replit Auth â†’ standard auth (Passport.js + JWT or similar)
OpenAI via Replit proxy â†’ Anthropic Claude API
PostgreSQL connection â€” needs new DATABASE_URL outside Replit

tier_1_monthly_broad_crawl.purpose: Discover all job boards, career pages, niche listings â€” build the address book
tier_1_monthly_broad_crawl.output: master_sources.json â€” prioritized URL/API endpoint list fed to daily scraper

tier_2_daily_delta_fetch.purpose: High-frequency extraction from known sources â€” new and updated listings only
tier_2_daily_delta_fetch.method: Check new/updated headers only â€” save bandwidth

deduplication_pipeline.stage_1: Normalize â€” standardize titles, salaries, locations
deduplication_pipeline.stage_2: Cluster â€” MinHash or similar to group near-duplicate descriptions across boards
deduplication_pipeline.stage_3: Head record â€” select most reputable source (direct company site preferred) as primary
deduplication_pipeline.stage_4: Collapse duplicates under head record with + expand UI

jit_dead_link_checker.trigger: Every time results are returned to user
jit_dead_link_checker.method: Async HEAD requests via httpx/aiohttp â€” ping all URLs simultaneously

logic.404_410: Silently flag as Stale in DB, remove from current view
logic.301_302: Follow redirect, update Head URL in DB
logic.403_429: Flag as Throttled â€” scraper may be blocked

heuristics: Multiple listings from different recruiters for same role with different pay = lead-gen funnel
Same job reposted every 3 days for 6+ months = ghost listing or scam
Domain reputation cross-reference against scam database
URL redirect chain > 3 hops = data funnel
Third-party aggregator vs direct company site â€” weight direct higher
scam_and_funnel_detection.action: Do NOT delete. Push to bottom tier, apply Suspect Listing flag

database_fields.original_source: Where first found in monthly crawl

stale_listing_feedback.applicant_sentiment: If 10+ users referred and none report response â€” flag as Stale/Unresponsive
stale_listing_feedback.age_threshold: Flag listings > 30 days old with no update

matching_engine.tier_1_high_match: Direct correlation between seeker internalTraits and job requirements

tier_2_limit_pushers.rule: If YearsExperience >= JobRequirement + 2 â†’ flag as Equivalent Match
tier_2_limit_pushers.display: Show to user with disclaimer: 'Matches structural requirements; specific credential may require upskilling'
tier_2_limit_pushers.flag: not_true_match â€” helps users explore stretch opportunities

geospatial_filter.local: Commute distance based on zip code â€” not just city name
geospatial_filter.remote: Verified Remote-First tag â€” separate bucket from local results

knowledge_match.example: User has 'programming logic background' + job requires 'Python' â†’ flag as Knowledge Match
knowledge_match.display: Matches structural logic requirements; specific syntax may require upskilling

session.handoff_note: Replit migration complete. Code is in Talent-Insight-Engine subfolder. Current Claude Code session is inventorying codebase. Migration priorities: (1) swap Replit Auth, (2) swap OpenAI proxy to Anthropic, (3) get DB running locally. Then build scraping engine starting with daily delta fetch.

decisions.question: Replit migration â€” confirmed complete?
options: Yes â€” Talent-Insight-Engine folder is in job-match folder
decisions.recommendation: Confirmed complete based on current Claude Code session finding the files
decisions.confidence_reason: Claude Code session found Talent-Insight-Engine subdirectory present

decisions.question: Auth migration â€” replace Replit Auth with what?
options: Simple session-based with bcrypt (no third party)
decisions.recommendation: Clerk â€” fastest to swap in, handles sessions/JWT/OAuth, generous free tier, minimal code change
decisions.confidence_reason: Clerk is the fastest Replit Auth replacement with least code rewrite. Auth0 is more powerful but overkill for current stage.

decisions.question: AI migration â€” keep GPT-4o or switch to Claude (Anthropic API)?
options: Run both â€” A/B test quality
decisions.recommendation: Switch to Claude Sonnet â€” you already have Anthropic API access via Claude Pro, no new account needed, narrative/behavioral analysis is a Claude strength
decisions.confidence_reason: Anthropic API already in your stack. Claude is strong at narrative interpretation which is core to this app.

decisions.question: Database â€” where does PostgreSQL run after leaving Replit?
decisions.recommendation: Supabase â€” free tier is generous, has good dashboard UI, and pairs well with your existing stack
decisions.confidence_reason: Free tier covers development and early production. Easy migration path from Replit's Postgres.

decisions.question: Scraping engine â€” start with monthly broad crawl or daily delta fetch first?
options: Monthly broad crawl first â€” build the address book
Daily delta fetch first â€” prove the pipeline works on known sources
decisions.recommendation: Daily delta fetch first â€” use known free sources (RemoteOK, We Work Remotely, Adzuna) to prove pipeline works before investing in broad crawl infrastructure
decisions.confidence_reason: Broad crawl is expensive and complex. Proving the pipeline on known sources de-risks the architecture before scaling up.

proactive_questions.question: Should the platform be offered as SaaS to other job seekers or stay personal/portfolio use?
proactive_questions.context: Determines whether to add billing, multi-user accounts, and marketing infrastructure. Significant scope difference.
proactive_questions.unlocks: Architecture decisions around multi-tenancy and billing

proactive_questions.question: Should employer portal be prioritized before or after scraping engine?
proactive_questions.context: Employer side is already partially built. Scraping engine is what feeds job seekers real data. Both are needed for a complete product.

proactive_questions.question: For the scam database â€” should it be private to your platform or eventually shared/open source?
proactive_questions.context: Shared scam database becomes a community moat. Private keeps it proprietary. Open source builds trust and contributions.
proactive_questions.unlocks: Scam database architecture and data sharing strategy

proactive_questions.question: Should job ratings be shown to users in v1 or held back until v2?
proactive_questions.context: Showing ratings adds UI complexity and requires calibrating the rating system. Hiding them keeps v1 cleaner but reduces user confidence in results.
proactive_questions.unlocks: Rating display UI and calibration decisions

autonomous_backlog.task: Complete codebase inventory â€” document all existing files, what each does, what works vs incomplete
Document Replit Auth dependency â€” identify every file that imports or uses replitAuth, map migration scope
Document OpenAI dependency â€” identify every API call, map to equivalent Anthropic Claude API calls
Research RemoteOK and We Work Remotely API/RSS endpoints â€” document structure, rate limits, fields returned
Research Adzuna API â€” free tier limits, endpoint structure, job field mapping
Design scam detection heuristic ruleset â€” document all criteria, scoring weights, action thresholds
Design deduplication pipeline â€” MinHash implementation spec, normalization rules, head record selection criteria
Design JIT dead-link checker â€” async HEAD request implementation, status code handling, DB update logic
Research Supabase free tier â€” setup requirements, Postgres migration from Replit, connection string format
Map matching engine logic â€” how internalTraits from narrative interview connect to job requirement fields

decision_log.decision: Do not scrape Indeed or LinkedIn
decision_log.reason: Both actively block scrapers. Risk of IP bans outweighs any data benefit.

decision_log.decision: Scam/low-quality listings are NOT deleted â€” pushed to bottom tier and flagged
decision_log.reason: Valid jobs can get caught in scam-adjacent patterns. Flagging preserves data integrity while protecting user experience.
Claude Code session confirmed Talent-Insight-Engine subdirectory present in job-match folder

assumption_log.assumption: Narrative/behavioral matching is more valuable differentiator than keyword matching
Daily delta fetch proves pipeline before investing in monthly broad crawl
Supabase or similar hosted Postgres replaces Replit database cleanly

reusable_findings.finding: Narrative interview + behavioral trait extraction pattern â€” applicable to Sentinel Performer personality clone intake

done_means: App runs locally without Replit environment
Auth works without Replit Auth
AI calls work via Anthropic API
Database connects via Supabase or equivalent
At least one source (RemoteOK or Adzuna) returns live job listings
Duplicate listings are collapsed under head record
JIT dead-link check runs before results displayed
Scam/suspect listings pushed to bottom with flag
Results display in existing frontend
Seeker completes narrative interview
AI generates profile with internalTraits
Profile matched against live job pool
Ranked results show with match tier labels
Dead links removed before display

completed: Scraping architecture fully designed from recovered conversation
blockers: Replit Auth must be replaced before app runs outside Replit
OpenAI proxy must be replaced with Anthropic API
session_log.next_action: Begin migration: (1) set up Supabase project + paste DATABASE_URL, (2) swap OpenAI calls to Anthropic Claude Sonnet, (3) install Clerk + replace Replit Auth. Then build daily delta scraper (RemoteOK, Adzuna).

strategy_notes: Narrative interview differentiator is genuinely unique â€” preserve and emphasize it
Migration unblocks everything â€” prioritize auth + database + AI swap before any new features
Scraping engine is the missing half â€” daily delta fetch first, monthly broad crawl second
Reusable finding: narrative intake pattern applies to Sentinel Performer personality clone

project.name: eBay Listing Optimizer
project.description: Bulk listing optimization engine that ingests raw inventory data, generates keyword-dense ATS-optimized titles, populates missing fields (condition, UPC/ISBN, category), and outputs eBay File Exchange ready CSV.
project.persona: eBay reseller (Joe, 572 active listings) who knows his inventory but doesn't have time to manually optimize every title and fill missing fields. Wants to drop a CSV in and get a better CSV out.

session.handoff_note: New project. No code yet. Joe has 572 active listings and deep title-writing expertise. First session: define input format, output format, and which optimizations to run.

decisions.question: Personal tool only or productize for other sellers?
options: Personal only â€” optimize Joe's 572 listings
decisions.recommendation: Personal first â€” prove it works on your own inventory, then decide
decisions.confidence_reason: Building for yourself first is faster and gives you real feedback before committing to multi-user architecture.

decisions.question: Input format â€” eBay bulk export CSV or manual entry or both?
decisions.recommendation: eBay bulk export CSV â€” you already have 572 listings to process
decisions.confidence_reason: CSV batch processing is the highest leverage use case given existing inventory size.

proactive_questions.question: What are your SKU prefix conventions for physical storage location tracking?
proactive_questions.context: Optimizer needs to preserve or understand existing SKU format to not break your storage system.

proactive_questions.question: Which categories make up the majority of your 572 listings?
proactive_questions.context: Category-specific title rules â€” e.g. books need ISBN, electronics need model numbers. Knowing top categories lets us build the right rules first.

proactive_questions.question: Should the optimizer suggest a price adjustment based on recent eBay sold data?
proactive_questions.context: Adds significant value but requires eBay API access. Could share that infrastructure with Arbitrage Pulse.
proactive_questions.unlocks: Pricing feature scope and eBay API integration

autonomous_backlog.task: Research eBay File Exchange CSV format â€” document all required and optional fields
Design title optimization rules â€” keyword density, character limit, banned words, category-specific patterns
Design missing field detection logic â€” which fields are commonly empty and how to auto-populate

assumption_log.assumption: eBay File Exchange format is the output target
AI-generated titles should preserve Joe's existing title style and conventions

reusable_findings.finding: eBay API for sold listings pricing â€” being researched for Arbitrage Pulse, share the integration

done_means: Accepts eBay export CSV as input
Generates optimized title for each listing
Flags missing condition, UPC/ISBN fields
Outputs valid eBay File Exchange CSV
Processes 50 listings without errors
Output titles are noticeably better than input titles

session_log.next_action: Answer input/output format decisions, get SKU convention info, then design optimization rules
